senses.gloss: denotes the time at which an occurrence started

examples.english: I have been seeing you since Thursday.

examples.text: رَأَيْتُكَ مُنْذُ يَوْمُ الْخَمِيسِ إِلَى يُوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ
examples.english: I saw you from Thursday to Friday.

senses.gloss: denotes an amount of time stretching from the past

examples.english: I have been seeing you for two days.

senses.gloss: denotes the length of the period between a time or occurrence in the past and the present

examples.text:
قِفَا نَبْكِ مِنْ ذِكْرَى حَبِيبٍ وَعِرْفَانِ
 وَرَسْمٍ عَفَتْ آيَاتُهُ مُنْذُ أَزْمَانِ
examples.english:
Halt! Let us weep
For the memory of
Our ladylove
And our memory of
A rubble heap,
Which faded away
So long ago.

examples.text: منذ زمن طويل وفي أراضٍ بعيدة، أنا آكو، المتحوّل الشرير، وسيد الظلام، أطلقت شرًا رهيبًا جدًا، لكنّ محارب ساموراي الغبيّ، يحمل سيفًا سحريًّا، تقدّم ليواجهني.
examples.english: Long ago in a distant land, I, Aku, the evil shape-shifter and the Master of Darkness, unleashed an unspeakable evil, but the foolish samurai warrior, who wields a magic sword, stepped forth to oppose me.

senses.gloss: denotes a time in the past

examples.english: I saw you back in a faraway time.

senses.gloss: denotes a time in the present

examples.text: مَا خَرَجَ مِنَ ٱلْبَيْتِ مُنْذُ نَزَلَ ٱلْمَطَرُ.
examples.english: He has not stepped out of the house since the rain set in.
Its name has been its glory since the time of [our] ancestors.
